item.title: First-year students – have you completed your transition survey?
item.description: The survey only takes ten minutes, and you'll be entered into a prize draw to win one of four £25 Love2shop vouchers.

item.title: International Microvolunteering Day
item.description: International Microvolunteering Day is celebrated every year on 15 April, and highlights how small actions can have a large impact on your community.

item.title: Thinking about where you’ll live next year?
item.description: University arranged accommodation isn't just for first years. If you're keen to be close to campus, avoid the hassle of sorting bills, and share a flat with friends, living in halls is a great option!

item.title: Celebrate Easter at NTU
item.description: Join NTU Faith and NTU Arts in the Global Lounges for two free Easter festival celebrations this March.

item.title: Introducing Denizen Records — NTU’s in-house record label
item.description: Did you know that NTU has an in-house record label? Denizen Records supports and develops students’ musical and industry skills.

item.title: Managing NTU’s digital storage limits
item.description: Over the next couple of months, we’ll be tidying up old versions of files across our Microsoft 365 platform. Find out what’s happening and how you can help manage your digital storage.

item.title: Knowing the signs of meningitis
item.description: Meningitis is a life-threatening infectious disease and students can be vulnerable to it due to close living arrangements.

item.title: Nottingham retains Purple Flag for safe and vibrant nightlife
item.description: Nottingham has been granted the Purple Flag award for the sixteenth consecutive year! The award recognises the diversity, vibrancy and safety of a night out in the city.

item.title: Neurodiversity Celebration Week 2026: Moving from Awareness to Action
item.description: Get involved in our range of events and help NTU move from awareness to meaningful action this Neurodiversity Celebration Week.

item.title: Your Voice. Your University. Your Students’ Union.
item.description: What should NTU look like in five years’ time? What kind of university should it become, and what should it be known for? Have your say now and you could win a £50 Love2shop voucher.
